title: Infosys Board Approves ₹ 18,000 Crore Equity Share Buyback
detailed_summary: Infosys Limited’s Board approved a proposed buyback of up to 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹ 5/- each for an aggregate amount of ₹ 18,000 Crore at ₹ 1,800/- per equity share, payable in cash. The shares represent up to 2.41% of the total number of equity shares in the existing paid-up equity share capital on a standalone basis. The buyback will be through the tender offer route from shareholders as on a record date to be announced later, subject to shareholder approval by remote e-voting and other applicable statutory approvals. The company also stated it will seek exemptive relief from the U.S. SEC because of conflicting Indian and U.S. tender offer requirements, and ADS holders may participate if they cancel ADSs and withdraw underlying equity shares before the record date.

key_facts.fact: The Board approved a proposal to buy back equity shares for an amount of ₹ 18,000 Crore at a price of ₹ 1,800/- per Equity Share, payable in cash.
The proposed buyback comprises 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value of ₹ 5/- each.
The proposed shares to be bought back represent up to 2.41% of the total number of Equity Shares in the existing total paid-up Equity Share capital of the Company on a standalone basis.
The buyback is proposed through the tender offer route from all equity shareholders as on a record date to be announced later.

The Buyback Size does not exceed 25% of the aggregate of the paid-up capital and free reserves, based on the latest audited interim condensed standalone and consolidated financial statements as on June 30, 2025.
The buyback has not yet been approved by shareholders and has not yet commenced.
key_facts.section_heading: Additional information pursuant to U.S. Law

key_facts.fact: Infosys stated it is necessary to seek and obtain exemptive relief from the U.S. SEC on certain aspects of the tender offer procedures because of conflicting Indian and U.S. regulatory requirements.
ADS holders may be able to participate in the buyback if they cancel ADSs and withdraw the underlying Equity Shares to become equity shareholders as on the Record Date.
The pre-buyback shareholding pattern as on September 05, 2025 showed Promoter and Promoter Group shareholding of 54,20,29,249 shares, or 13.05%.
key_facts.section_heading: Annexure A Pre-Buyback Shareholding Pattern of the Company as on September 05, 2025

key_facts.fact: The pre-buyback total share capital shown in the shareholding pattern was 4,15,43,92,203 shares.
key_facts.section_heading: Annexure A Pre-Buyback Shareholding Pattern of the Company as on September 05, 2025

corporate_actions.description: Infosys approved a proposal to buy back up to 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹ 5/- each through the tender offer route from equity shareholders as on a record date to be announced later.
corporate_actions.amount_or_ratio: ₹ 18,000 Crore; ₹ 1,800/- per Equity Share; up to 2.41% of the total number of Equity Shares

litigation_or_regulatory_matters.description: Infosys stated it will seek exemptive relief from the U.S. SEC on certain aspects of tender offer procedures because of conflicting regulatory requirements between Indian and U.S. laws for tender offer buybacks.
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.status: The Company will be making application in this regard.

risk_factors.risk: The buyback had not yet been approved by shareholders and had not yet commenced.
risk_factors.potential_impact: The buyback remains subject to shareholder approval and applicable statutory approvals.
